zh.lifeSummary: 當Jean de Beaugency seigneur de La Flèche於1030年11月1日出生在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易薩特拉 福利艾時，他的父親Lancelin II de Beaugency-9歲，母親Alberge du Maine-7歲。他和Paula du Maine至少有7個兒子。他去世於1097年在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易薩特享年67歲，埋葬在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易麥-吉洛易安格斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jean de Beaugency seigneur de La Flèche was born on 1 November 1030, in La Flèche, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, France, his father, Lancelin II de Beaugency, was -9 and his mother, Alberge du Maine, was -7. He had at least 7 sons with Paula du Maine. He died in 1097, in Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, France, at the age of 67, and was buried in Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jean de Beaugency seigneur de La Flèche am 1. November 1030 in La Flèche, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Lancelin II de Beaugency -9 und seine Mutter Alberge du Maine -7. Er hatte mit Paula du Maine mindestens 7 Söhne. Er starb 1097 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Frankreich und wurde in Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Frankreich begraben.

ja.Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, France: フランスペイズ ド ラ ロイアーサルス
ja.lifeSummary: Jean de Beaugency seigneur de La Flècheが1030年11月1日にフランスペイズ ド ラ ロイアーサルスラ フルeチェで生まれた時，父Lancelin II de Beaugencyは-9歳，母Alberge du Maineは-7歳であった。彼はPaula du Maineとの間に少なくとも7人の息子をもうけた。彼は1097年にフランスペイズ ド ラ ロイアーサルスで67歳で死去し，フランスペイズ ド ラ ロイアーメインエ-エト-ロイアーアンジャーズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jean de Beaugency seigneur de La Flèche(이)가 1030년 11월 1일 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 사르테 라 플e체에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lancelin II de Beaugency(은)는 -9세였고, 그의 어머니 Alberge du Maine(은)는 -7세였다. 그는 Paula du Maine(와)과 적어도 일곱 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1097년 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 사르테에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르 앤그르스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jean de Beaugency seigneur de La Flèche nasceu em 1 de novembro de 1030, em La Flèche, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, França, seu pai, Lancelin II de Beaugency, tinha -9 anos e sua mãe, Alberge du Maine, tinha -7 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 7 filhos com Paula du Maine. Ele faleceu em 1097, em Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, França, com 67 anos, e foi sepultado em Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jean de Beaugency seigneur de La Flèche родился 1 ноября 1030 г. в местности ла флeч, сарт, пеис де ла лоир, Франция, его отцу по имени Lancelin II de Beaugency было -9 год, а матери, которую звали Alberge du Maine, -7. У него было по крайней мере 7 сыновей от Paula du Maine. Он умер в 1097 г. в местности сарт, пеис де ла лоир, Франция в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности ангирс, меине-ет-лоир, пеис де ла лоир, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jean de Beaugency seigneur de La Flèche nació el 1 de noviembre de 1030, en La Flèche, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Francia, su papá, Lancelin II de Beaugency, tenía -9 años y su mamá, Alberge du Maine, tenía -7 años. Tuvo por lo menos 7 hijos con Paula du Maine. Murió en 1097, en Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Francia, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultado en Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Francia.
